name: always_false
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_false(pm: &ProcessManager, ancestor_ptr: ContainerPtr, child_ptr: ContainerPtr)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(ancestor_ptr),
        pm.container_dom().contains(child_ptr),
    ensures
        !pm.get_container(ancestor_ptr).subtree_set@.contains(child_ptr),
{
}
reason: If subtree_set is always empty the ancestor check is vacuously false, breaking all hierarchy queries

name: always_true
code:
proof fn phi_2_always_true(pm: &ProcessManager, ancestor_ptr: ContainerPtr, child_ptr: ContainerPtr)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(ancestor_ptr),
        pm.container_dom().contains(child_ptr),
    ensures
        pm.get_container(ancestor_ptr).subtree_set@.contains(child_ptr),
{
}
reason: If every container is trivially an ancestor of every other, isolation between containers is completely broken

name: symmetric_ancestry
code:
proof fn phi_3_symmetric_ancestry(pm: &ProcessManager, a: ContainerPtr, b: ContainerPtr)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(a),
        pm.container_dom().contains(b),
        a != b,
        pm.get_container(a).subtree_set@.contains(b),
    ensures
        pm.get_container(b).subtree_set@.contains(a),
{
}
reason: Ancestry in a tree must be antisymmetric; symmetry would mean every ancestor relationship is cyclic, destroying the tree structure

name: deeper_node_is_ancestor
code:
proof fn phi_4_deeper_node_is_ancestor(pm: &ProcessManager, ancestor_ptr: ContainerPtr, child_ptr: ContainerPtr)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(ancestor_ptr),
        pm.container_dom().contains(child_ptr),
        ancestor_ptr != child_ptr,
        pm.get_container(ancestor_ptr).depth >= pm.get_container(child_ptr).depth,
    ensures
        pm.get_container(ancestor_ptr).subtree_set@.contains(child_ptr),
{
}
reason: A strictly-deeper-or-equal node cannot be a proper ancestor of a shallower node; if the spec allows this the depth-based early-return optimization is unsound

name: same_depth_implies_ancestor
code:
proof fn phi_5_same_depth_implies_ancestor(pm: &ProcessManager, a: ContainerPtr, b: ContainerPtr)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(a),
        pm.container_dom().contains(b),
        a != b,
        pm.get_container(a).depth == pm.get_container(b).depth,
    ensures
        pm.get_container(a).subtree_set@.contains(b),
{
}
reason: Distinct containers at the same depth in a tree are siblings or cousins and cannot be ancestors of each other; entailment would indicate a broken depth invariant
